key_insight: An AI-guided proteomics workflow identified a simple two-protein biomarker panel, PGC and DNMT1, that can distinguish gastric-type cervical adenocarcinoma from HPV-related cervical cancer.
clinical_relevance: This could improve diagnostic accuracy for a rare, aggressive cervical cancer that is often misdiagnosed, and PGC may also help with prognosis.
method: Multi-center proteomic profiling of 407 cervical tissue samples
Generative AI created synthetic proteomic profiles to augment training data
Biologically informed network analysis prioritized interpretable biomarker candidates
evidence_signal: The two-protein signature achieved 93% accuracy in the test cohort and 97% in an external proteomic cohort, with 87.9% accuracy in IHC validation.
watch_item: The biomarker panel needs broader prospective validation before routine clinical use, especially given the rarity and diagnostic complexity of GAS.
paper_title: AI-enabled proteomic profiling identifies interpretable diagnostic biomarkers for gastric-type adenocarcinoma of the uterine cervix
